creator: cracauer@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: open->closed

Maciej, thanks for the bug report. 

However, it is not a bug. 

Rdate does not use the ntp protocol, what you are trying to do is 
intended to work.  You need a regular time server port open (usually 
via inetd) on the server. 

What you want to do is done with `ntpdate <servernames>`. 

Many public ntp servers also have the regular time server port open so 
that `rdate <randomntpserver>` often works, but that is a conincidence. 

Thanks 
Martin
